enrichment.short_description: Vertice is an AI-powered procurement platform that automates SaaS purchasing, negotiation, and spend management.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vertice review: AI procurement platform for intake-to-procure workflows, SaaS purchasing negotiation, and vendor spend benchmarking.

features.title: Intake-to-Procure
features.description: Automated request capture, routing, and approval workflows for procurement requests.

features.title: SaaS Purchasing
features.description: Expert-led negotiation support backed by pricing benchmarks across 32,000+ vendors.

features.title: Vertice AI
features.description: AI agents trained to complete procurement tasks such as intake triage and vendor research.

features.title: Vendor Management
features.description: Vendor onboarding and supplier tracking in a central system of record.

features.title: Contract Management
features.description: Risk identification and renewal tracking across the contract lifecycle.

features.title: Budget and Risk Management
features.description: Modules for tracking departmental budgets and flagging procurement risk.

pros: Pricing benchmark data spans 32,000+ vendors for negotiation leverage
Combines workflow automation with human-led SaaS negotiation services
Expanded scale after acquiring Vendr
Used by companies including Santander, The AA, and ClearScore
cons: No public pricing; requires booking a demo to get a quote
Aimed at mid-to-large enterprise procurement teams, not solo buyers
Savings and ROI figures are Vertice's own marketing claims

faqs.question: What is Vertice?
faqs.answer: Vertice is an AI-powered procurement platform that automates purchasing workflows and combines SaaS spend benchmarking with expert-led vendor negotiation.

faqs.question: Does Vertice publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: No. Vertice does not list public pricing; prospective customers need to book a demo or contact sales for a quote.

faqs.question: Has Vertice acquired other companies?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vertice has acquired Vendr, another SaaS spend management provider, expanding its scale.

faqs.question: Where is Vertice based?
faqs.answer: Vertice is headquartered in London, UK, with additional offices including New York, Sydney, Johannesburg, Brno, and Linz.

use_cases.title: SaaS spend negotiation
use_cases.description: Finance teams use Vertice's benchmarking data and negotiation service to reduce software costs.

use_cases.title: Centralizing purchase intake
use_cases.description: Procurement teams route and approve purchase requests through a single intake-to-procure workflow.

use_cases.title: Vendor risk tracking
use_cases.description: IT and security teams monitor vendor contracts for renewal dates and risk exposure.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Vertice?
paragraphs: Vertice is a procurement platform that automates purchasing workflows from intake through completion, combining request routing and approvals with vendor pricing benchmarks and contract management.

The platform pairs its software with a human negotiation service for SaaS purchases, using pricing data drawn from more than 32,000 vendors to help finance and procurement teams reduce software spend.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Vertice is for
paragraphs: Vertice is built for finance, procurement, IT, security, and legal teams at mid-to-large enterprises that manage significant SaaS and cloud spend and want centralized intake, contract tracking, and negotiation support.

enrichment.short_description: PayEm is a global spend and procurement platform combining PO creation, AP automation, and corporate cards for finance teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: PayEm review: global spend and procurement platform with PO creation, AI invoice matching, corporate cards, and ERP integrations.

features.title: PO creation
features.description: Automates and customizes request and approval workflows, then creates purchase orders synced with the ERP.

features.title: AP automation
features.description: Uses AI-powered OCR to match incoming invoices against their corresponding purchase orders automatically.

features.title: Corporate cards
features.description: Issues virtual and physical corporate cards, including dedicated vendor cards for SaaS subscriptions.

features.title: Spend controls
features.description: Sets spending limits, enforces policies, and monitors transactions in real time.

features.title: ERP integrations
features.description: Syncs with ERPs such as NetSuite to keep procurement and accounting data aligned.

features.title: Approval workflows in Slack and email
features.description: Routes requests and approvals through Slack and email in addition to the PayEm platform.

features.title: Cross-border payments
features.description: Supports cross-border payment and reconciliation for global vendor relationships.

pros: Unifies procurement, AP automation, and corporate cards in one platform
AI-powered OCR automatically matches invoices to purchase orders
Dedicated vendor cards give control over individual SaaS subscriptions
Syncs directly with NetSuite and other ERPs
cons: No public pricing; requires a sales conversation to get a quote
Aimed at company-wide procurement and finance teams rather than solo users
Website provides limited self-serve product documentation

faqs.question: What is PayEm?
faqs.answer: PayEm is a global spend and procurement platform that automates the request-to-reconciliation process, combining purchase orders, AP automation, and corporate cards.

faqs.question: Does PayEm integrate with ERPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, PayEm syncs purchase orders and spend data with ERPs such as NetSuite.

faqs.question: Does PayEm use AI?
faqs.answer: Yes, PayEm uses AI-powered OCR to automatically match invoices against their corresponding purchase orders.

faqs.question: Where is PayEm based?
faqs.answer: PayEm is headquartered in New York, NY, and was founded in 2019.

use_cases.title: Automating purchase orders
use_cases.description: Procurement teams route requests through approval workflows that automatically generate ERP-synced POs.

use_cases.title: AP invoice matching
use_cases.description: Finance and AP teams use AI OCR to match vendor invoices to open purchase orders and reconcile spend.

use_cases.title: Controlling SaaS vendor spend
use_cases.description: Teams issue a dedicated virtual card per vendor to cap and track individual subscription costs.
use_cases.audience: IT and finance teams managing SaaS spend

long_form_blocks.heading: What is PayEm?
paragraphs: PayEm is a global spend and procurement platform that automates the request-to-reconciliation process for finance and procurement teams, combining purchase order creation, AP automation, and corporate cards in one system.
The platform uses AI-powered OCR to match invoices against purchase orders automatically, and it syncs with ERPs such as NetSuite while routing approvals through Slack and email.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who PayEm is for
paragraphs: PayEm suits finance and procurement teams that want to consolidate purchase requests, invoice matching, vendor cards, and ERP-synced spend data into a single workflow.

enrichment.short_description: Soldo is a UK-based spend management platform offering company cards, bank transfers, and automated expense tracking.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Soldo review: spend management with physical and virtual company cards, OCR receipt capture, and real-time budget controls.

features.title: Spend limits and approvals
features.description: Sets proactive spending limits and approval workflows before money leaves the business.

features.title: Cards and transfers
features.description: Issues physical and virtual debit/prepaid cards and supports outbound bank transfers.

features.title: OCR receipt capture
features.description: Mobile app automatically captures and matches receipts to transactions using OCR.

features.title: Real-time reporting
features.description: Gives finance teams real-time visibility into spend and budgets across teams.

features.title: Accounting integrations
features.description: Automates reconciliation with integrations into major accounting platforms.

features.title: Multi-entity hierarchy
features.description: Manages wallets, teams, and role-based access across multiple entities or locations.

pros: Regulated as an Electronic Money Institution by the UK Financial Conduct Authority
Serves 25,000+ businesses across 31 European countries
Rated 'Excellent' on G2, Capterra, and Trustpilot
Independent Forrester study found 358% ROI with under 6-month payback
cons: Entry-level Standard plan is capped at 3 users and 3 cards
Unlimited plan requires a custom quote rather than listed pricing
Pricing is quoted in GBP plus VAT, which may complicate budgeting outside the UK/EU

faqs.question: What is Soldo?
faqs.answer: Soldo is a spend management platform that combines company cards, bank transfers, and automated expense tracking with real-time budget controls.

faqs.question: How much does Soldo cost?
faqs.answer: Soldo's Standard plan starts at £21/month + VAT, Plus is £33/month + VAT, and Unlimited requires a custom quote.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Soldo offers a free 30-day trial across its plans.

faqs.question: Is Soldo regulated?
faqs.answer: Yes, Soldo is regulated as an Electronic Money Institution by the UK's Financial Conduct Authority, with additional entities in Italy and Ireland.

use_cases.title: Decentralized team spending
use_cases.description: Finance leaders issue cards with preset limits to teams or locations while keeping central control.

use_cases.title: Automated receipt reconciliation
use_cases.description: Employees capture receipts via mobile app, which Soldo matches to card transactions using OCR.

use_cases.title: Multi-entity budget management
use_cases.description: Organizations with multiple entities or countries manage separate wallets and role-based access.

features: Basic spend management and receipt capture
Role-based access and controls at scale

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Soldo?
paragraphs: Soldo is a spend management platform that gives businesses physical and virtual company cards alongside bank transfers, with proactive spending limits and approval workflows to control company spend before it happens.
The platform automates expense capture through OCR receipt scanning, provides real-time reporting, and is regulated as an Electronic Money Institution by the UK's Financial Conduct Authority.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Soldo is for
paragraphs: Soldo is aimed at CFOs, finance leaders, and operations teams at small-to-enterprise businesses that need to decentralize spending across teams or locations while retaining central visibility and control.

enrichment.short_description: Found is a US business banking platform combining checking, bookkeeping, tax estimates, and contractor payments for the self-employed.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Found review: free business checking with automatic bookkeeping, tax write-offs, invoicing, and contractor payments.

features.title: Business checking
features.description: Online business checking account with a Mastercard business debit card, check deposits, and wires.

features.title: Automatic bookkeeping
features.description: Categorizes expenses automatically and generates financial reports with custom tags and rules.

features.title: Tax tools
features.description: Calculates automatic write-offs, tax estimates, and generates tax forms including 1099-NEC filings.

features.title: Contractor payments
features.description: Pays an unlimited number of contractors with no per-contractor fees and collects W-9s automatically.

features.title: Team cards
features.description: Issues team debit cards with spend controls for up to 10 physical and 20 virtual cards.

features.title: AI assistant
features.description: A beta AI feature lets users query their finances and delegate certain financial tasks.

pros: Free Core plan with no monthly fee, minimum balance, or credit check
Combines banking, bookkeeping, and tax tools in a single app
Unlimited contractor payments with no per-contractor fees and free 1099-NEC generation
Rated 4.8 on the App Store (26K+ ratings) and 4.7 on Google Play (16K+ reviews)
cons: Banking services are provided by a third-party bank (Lead Bank), not Found directly
Higher APY, cash back, and a dedicated account manager require the paid Found Plus or Pro tier
AI assistant is still in beta

faqs.question: Is Found free to use?
faqs.answer: Found's Core plan is free, with optional paid Found Plus ($35/month) and Found Pro ($80/month) tiers for higher interest rates and extra features.

faqs.question: Does Found charge fees for paying contractors?
faqs.answer: No, Found offers unlimited contractor payments with no per-contractor fees and free 1099-NEC generation.

faqs.question: Is Found a bank?
faqs.answer: No, Found is a financial technology company; banking services are provided by Lead Bank, a member FDIC institution.

faqs.question: What tax tools does Found include?
faqs.answer: Found automatically tracks write-offs, estimates taxes, tracks mileage, and generates tax forms including 1099-NEC filings for contractors.

use_cases.title: Consolidating banking and bookkeeping
use_cases.description: Self-employed business owners manage checking, expense categorization, and reporting in one app.

use_cases.title: Tax preparation
use_cases.description: Freelancers track automatic write-offs and quarterly tax estimates throughout the year.

use_cases.title: Paying contractors
use_cases.description: Small businesses pay an unlimited number of contractors and generate 1099-NEC forms without per-payment fees.

features: Contractor payments with no per-contractor fees
2.50% APY on all balances, no cap
1% cash back on qualifying card transactions

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Found?
paragraphs: Found is a business banking platform that pairs an online business checking account with built-in bookkeeping, tax estimation, invoicing, and contractor payment tools in a single app.

The free Core plan includes checking, automatic expense categorization, and unlimited contractor payments, while paid Found Plus and Found Pro tiers add higher APY, cash back, and a dedicated account manager.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Found is for
paragraphs: Found is built for self-employed individuals, freelancers, and small business owners who want banking, bookkeeping, and tax preparation combined into one platform instead of stitching together separate tools.

enrichment.short_description: Lili is a US business banking platform offering fee-free checking, high-yield savings, and integrated bookkeeping tools.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lili review: no-fee business checking, high-yield savings up to 4.00% APY, and built-in bookkeeping and tax tools.

features.title: Business checking
features.description: Core account with no monthly fee and a Visa business debit card.

features.title: High-yield savings
features.description: Savings feature offering up to 4.00% APY on balances up to $1M with no minimums or lockups.

features.title: Extended FDIC coverage
features.description: FDIC insurance up to $3M through a sweep network of partner banks.

features.title: Payments
features.description: Express ACH transfers and international wires in 130+ currencies.

features.title: Business credit
features.description: Access to business lines of credit up to $250,000.

features.title: Bookkeeping and invoicing
features.description: Built-in invoicing, bill pay, bookkeeping, and tax preparation tools.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with QuickBooks, Xero, Stripe, Shopify, and Gusto.

pros: Core checking account has no monthly fee
Serves over 200,000 businesses
Rated 4.7/5 on Trustpilot from 3,900+ reviews
Live customer support available 7 days a week
cons: Pricing for the paid Pro, Smart, and Premium tiers is not publicly disclosed
Banking services are provided by a partner bank (Sunrise Banks, N.A.), not Lili directly
Specific savings APY tiers and requirements are not fully detailed on the homepage

faqs.question: Is Lili a bank?
faqs.answer: No, Lili is a financial technology company; banking services are provided by Sunrise Banks, N.A., a member FDIC institution.

faqs.question: Does Lili charge monthly fees?
faqs.answer: The Core account has no monthly fee; Pro, Smart, and Premium tiers carry fees that are not disclosed on the homepage.

faqs.question: How much can I earn with Lili savings?
faqs.answer: Lili advertises up to 4.00% APY on savings balances up to $1M with no minimums or lockups.

faqs.question: What accounting tools does Lili integrate with?
faqs.answer: Lili integrates with QuickBooks, Xero, Stripe, Shopify, and Gusto.

use_cases.title: Fee-free business banking
use_cases.description: Small business owners open a no-monthly-fee checking account with a Visa debit card.

use_cases.title: Earning yield on cash reserves
use_cases.description: Businesses hold operating cash in a high-yield savings account with extended FDIC coverage.

use_cases.title: International payments
use_cases.description: Businesses send international wires in 130+ currencies alongside domestic ACH transfers.
use_cases.audience: Businesses with international vendors or clients

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lili?
paragraphs: Lili is a business banking platform offering a no-fee Core checking account alongside high-yield savings, extended FDIC coverage, and access to business lines of credit up to $250,000.
The platform also includes invoicing, bill pay, bookkeeping, and tax preparation tools, with integrations into QuickBooks, Xero, Stripe, Shopify, and Gusto.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lili is for
paragraphs: Lili is designed for small business owners and entrepreneurs, from solopreneurs to scaling companies, who want fee-free banking combined with bookkeeping and tax tools in one platform.

name: North One
skip_reason: north one's website (northone.com) 301-redirects to relayfi.com, a fully rebranded product called 'Relay' with no mention of 'North One' anywhere on the live site — treating this as a discontinued/renamed brand rather than publishing content under a defunct name.

enrichment.short_description: Bluevine is a US digital business banking platform offering fee-free checking, tiered APY, and lines of credit.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Bluevine review: business checking with up to 3.0% APY, lines of credit up to $250,000, and invoicing with Tap to Pay.

features.title: Business checking
features.description: Digital checking account with tiered APY and no monthly fee on the Standard plan.

features.title: Lines of credit
features.description: Access to business lines of credit up to $250,000 or term loans up to $500,000 through lending partners.

features.title: Invoicing and payment links
features.description: Manage invoices and accept payments via shareable payment links.

features.title: Tap to Pay
features.description: In-person card payments powered by Stripe.

features.title: Accounts payable automation
features.description: Automates money movement and bill payments for the business.

features.title: Security
features.description: Two-factor authentication, data encryption, and real-time fraud alerts.

pros: Standard plan has no monthly fee
Premier plan offers up to 3.0% APY with no balance cap
FDIC insurance protection up to $3 million
Better Business Bureau A+ rating
cons: Plus and Premier plans carry monthly fees ($30 and $95) unless waived
Standard plan's APY requires meeting card spend or deposit thresholds
Lending products are issued through third-party bank partners, not Bluevine directly

faqs.question: Does Bluevine charge monthly fees?
faqs.answer: The Standard plan has no monthly fee; the Plus ($30/month) and Premier ($95/month) plans carry fees that can be waived under certain conditions.

faqs.question: What interest rate does Bluevine offer?
faqs.answer: Bluevine offers tiered APY ranging from 1.3% on the Standard plan up to 3.0% uncapped on the Premier plan.

faqs.question: Can I get a business loan through Bluevine?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bluevine offers lines of credit up to $250,000 or term loans up to $500,000 through lending partners.

faqs.question: Does Bluevine support in-person payments?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bluevine offers Tap to Pay for in-person card payments, powered by Stripe.

use_cases.title: Fee-free checking with interest
use_cases.description: Small businesses open a no-monthly-fee checking account that earns tiered interest.

use_cases.title: Accessing working capital
use_cases.description: Businesses apply for a line of credit or term loan to fund growth or cover cash flow gaps.

use_cases.title: Accepting in-person payments
use_cases.description: Businesses use Tap to Pay to accept card payments in person via Stripe.

features: 1.3% APY up to $250,000 in eligible balances
Requires $500/month card spend or $2,500/month in customer payments
1.75% APY up to $250,000, no eligibility requirements
3.0% APY on all balances, no cap

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Bluevine?
paragraphs: Bluevine is a digital business banking platform offering checking accounts with tiered interest rates, lines of credit and term loans through lending partners, invoicing, and in-person payment acceptance via Tap to Pay.
The Standard plan has no monthly fee, while Plus and Premier plans raise the available APY in exchange for a monthly fee that can be waived under certain conditions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Bluevine is for
paragraphs: Bluevine targets small businesses in the United States looking for a digital-first alternative to traditional business banking, with access to working capital and interest-earning checking.

enrichment.short_description: Arc is a cash management and capital markets platform for technology companies, offering yield, cards, and debt financing.
enrichment.headquarters: San Francisco, CA and New York, NY
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Arc review: cash management platform for tech companies with brokerage yield, a business card, and AI-powered CFO tools.

features.title: Cash management
features.description: Treasury dashboard for managing operating cash and brokerage balances in one place.

features.title: Yield on cash
features.description: Brokerage net yield ranging up to roughly 4.6% depending on the membership tier.

features.title: Business card
features.description: Card with cashback rewards that scale from 1.0% to 2.0% by tier.

features.title: Debt capital raising
features.description: Marketplace and tools for raising venture debt directly through the platform.

features.title: CFO Agent
features.description: AI-powered assistant for financial tasks, with unlimited access on the Premium tier.

pros: Free Essentials tier requires no paid membership to start
Enterprise tier offers up to roughly 4.57% net yield and 2.0% cashback
Unlimited AI CFO Agent access included on the Premium tier
Combines cash management, a business card, and debt fundraising in one platform
cons: Higher yield and cashback tiers require a paid annual membership
Axos Financial announced an agreement to acquire Arc in July 2026, which may bring platform changes
Specific account minimums and fees are not disclosed on the pricing page

faqs.question: What is Arc?
faqs.answer: Arc is a cash management and capital markets platform for technology companies, offering brokerage yield, a business card, debt capital raising, and AI-powered financial tools.

faqs.question: Is Arc free to use?
faqs.answer: Arc offers a free Essentials tier, with paid Premium and Enterprise tiers unlocking higher yield, more cashback, and dedicated support.

faqs.question: Is Arc being acquired?
faqs.answer: Yes, Axos Financial announced a definitive agreement in July 2026 to acquire Arc Technologies, Inc.

faqs.question: What is Arc's CFO Agent?
faqs.answer: CFO Agent is Arc's AI-powered financial assistant; Premium members get unlimited access.

use_cases.title: Managing idle cash for yield
use_cases.description: Tech companies hold operating cash in Arc's brokerage sweep to earn a net yield instead of sitting idle.

use_cases.title: Raising venture debt
use_cases.description: Finance teams use Arc's debt marketplace to raise capital directly through the platform.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted finance tasks
use_cases.description: Teams use the CFO Agent to handle financial tasks and questions through an AI assistant.

features: Up to 4.12% net yield on brokerage
Core cash management and treasury features
Up to 4.52% net yield on brokerage
Up to 4.57% net yield on brokerage

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Arc?
paragraphs: Arc is a cash management and capital markets platform built for technology companies, combining brokerage yield on idle cash, a business card, and tools for raising venture debt in one dashboard.

Arc offers a free Essentials tier plus paid Premium and Enterprise membership tiers that unlock higher yield, more cashback, and access to its AI-powered CFO Agent. In July 2026, Axos Financial announced a definitive agreement to acquire Arc.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Arc is for
paragraphs: Arc is aimed at startups and technology companies that want to earn yield on operating cash, raise debt capital, and get AI-assisted financial support without stitching together multiple treasury tools.

enrichment.short_description: Pipe is an embedded financial operating system that lets software platforms offer capital and payment tools to their business customers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pipe review: embedded financial OS letting SaaS platforms offer their customers capital based on transaction data.

features.title: Embedded capital offers
features.description: Generates pre-approved capital offers for platform customers based on their transaction data.

features.title: Sales-based repayment
features.description: Repayment scales with sales activity, with no fixed monthly minimums.

features.title: Low-code embedding
features.description: Integrates into a partner's product with a small number of code snippets.

features.title: Industry-specific risk models
features.description: Tailors underwriting to different platform types, including vertical SaaS, payment facilitators, and marketplaces.

features.title: Partner analytics
features.description: Reports activation rate, conversion rate, and customer growth metrics to partners.

pros: Reports a 61% average conversion rate and an 80 Net Promoter Score across partners
Used by known partners including Uber Eats, GoCardless, and Housecall Pro
Sales-based repayment avoids fixed monthly minimums for end customers
Designed for quick embedding with minimal code
cons: Pricing is not publicly disclosed
Built for platforms to embed, not something an individual business signs up for directly
Requires a partnership and sales engagement to implement

faqs.question: What is Pipe?
faqs.answer: Pipe is an embedded financial operating system that lets software platforms offer capital and payment tools to their business customers.

faqs.question: Who uses Pipe?
faqs.answer: Pipe is used by vertical SaaS platforms, payment facilitators, and marketplaces, with partners including Uber Eats, GoCardless, and Housecall Pro.

faqs.question: How does repayment work?
faqs.answer: Pipe's capital product uses sales-based repayment tied to a business's transaction volume, with no fixed monthly minimums.

faqs.question: Where is Pipe headquartered?
faqs.answer: Pipe Technologies Inc. is headquartered in Miami, Florida, and was founded in 2019.

use_cases.title: Embedding capital in a SaaS product
use_cases.description: Vertical SaaS platforms embed Pipe's capital offers directly into their product for customers.

use_cases.title: Boosting payment facilitator retention
use_cases.description: Payment facilitators offer embedded financial tools to increase customer retention and revenue.

use_cases.title: Marketplace seller financing
use_cases.description: Marketplaces offer embedded capital to sellers to increase loyalty and platform usage.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pipe?
paragraphs: Pipe is an embedded financial operating system that software platforms integrate into their own products so they can offer capital and payment tools to their business customers.

The platform generates pre-approved capital offers based on a customer's transaction data, with sales-based repayment and no fixed monthly minimums, and it applies industry-specific risk models for vertical SaaS, payment facilitators, and marketplaces.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pipe is for
paragraphs: Pipe is built for software platforms — vertical SaaS products, payment facilitators, and marketplaces — that want to embed financial tools for their end customers rather than for individual businesses signing up directly.

enrichment.short_description: Capchase is a vendor financing platform that pays B2B tech vendors upfront while buyers pay for software over time.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Capchase review: vendor financing for B2B tech, with instant underwriting and Salesforce-embedded deal workflows.

features.title: Instant underwriting
features.description: Decisions roughly 97% of applications within 30 seconds.

features.title: Salesforce integration
features.description: Embeds buyer qualification and loan document generation directly into Salesforce.

features.title: Flexible payment terms
features.description: Offers monthly, quarterly, or extended installment terms for buyers.

features.title: Multi-party deal management
features.description: Collaboration portal for deals involving vendors, buyers, and channel partners.

features.title: Automated billing
features.description: Automates billing and offers buy-now-pay-later style options for financed purchases.

features.title: Multi-currency coverage
features.description: Supports financing across 9 countries in multiple currencies.

pros: Fast underwriting, with most applications decisioned within 30 seconds
Embeds directly into Salesforce sales workflows
Supports multi-currency financing across 9 countries
Reports a 25% increase in client deal win rates
cons: Pricing is not publicly disclosed
Financing is aimed at enterprise vendors and buyers, not individual consumers
Requires sales engagement to set up vendor financing

faqs.question: What is Capchase?
faqs.answer: Capchase is a vendor financing platform that lends directly to buyers and pays B2B tech vendors upfront, letting buyers pay over time.

faqs.question: How fast is Capchase's underwriting?
faqs.answer: Capchase states that about 97% of applications receive a decision within 30 seconds.

faqs.question: Does Capchase integrate with Salesforce?
faqs.answer: Yes, Capchase embeds buyer qualification and loan document generation into Salesforce.

faqs.question: Where is Capchase based?
faqs.answer: Capchase was founded in Boston in 2020 and is now headquartered in New York, NY, with additional offices.

use_cases.title: Increasing deal velocity
use_cases.description: Sales teams offer buyers financing to close enterprise deals faster.

use_cases.title: Getting paid upfront
use_cases.description: Finance departments receive upfront payment on contracts that buyers pay off over time.
use_cases.audience: Finance teams at software or hardware vendors

use_cases.title: Multi-party deal financing
use_cases.description: Channel partners and resellers manage financed deals involving multiple parties through a shared portal.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Capchase?
paragraphs: Capchase is a vendor financing platform for B2B software and hardware purchases: it lends directly to buyers, pays vendors upfront, and lets buyers repay over flexible monthly, quarterly, or extended terms.

The platform embeds into Salesforce for buyer qualification and loan document generation, offers instant underwriting decisions, and supports multi-party deals with channel partners and resellers across 9 countries.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Capchase is for
paragraphs: Capchase is built for B2B tech vendor sales and finance teams that want to close larger deals faster by offering buyers financing, while still getting paid in full upfront.
